title: Cheap Drones Remain Wild Card in Iran war
summary: Stopping Iran’s production of drones is critical to opening the Strait of Hormuz and halting its attacks on Gulf nations. But can it be done?

title: An Increase in U.S. Troops to the Middle East, and a String of Attacks on Jewish Sites
summary: Plus, a jaw-dropping A.I. tool goes dark.

title: Air Canada Pilots Killed in LaGuardia Crash Were Early in Flying Careers
summary: The pilots were identified as Antoine Forest, 30, and Mackenzie Gunther. They were the only two fatalities in the plane’s collision with a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport.

title: Ukraine Finally Got Battlefield Momentum. Now Comes a Russian Offensive.
summary: Moscow’s forces are intensifying their attacks in southern Ukraine after Kyiv made rare gains along the front.

title: An American in Russia Is Linked to Neo-Nazi Terror Cells Across Europe
summary: F.B.I. agents thought they had weakened an online hate group known as the Base. A string of European terrorism cases indicates it has resurged.

title: Democrat Emily Gregory Wins Florida Special Election in Mar-a-Lago’s District
summary: Emily Gregory’s victory in Palm Beach brought the Democratic surge to President Trump’s backyard, while a union leader leads in a race for a state senate seat vacated by Florida’s lieutenant governor.

title: The 7 Biggest Decluttering Myths
summary: Experts said these were the most commonly held beliefs about organization — and they’re holding you back.

title: A Day in the Life of a New York City Junklugger
summary: The work force behind Junkluggers, a company that tosses, donates and resells what we leave behind, bears witness to grief and lives in transition.

title: In San Jose, a Reckoning Over Cesar Chavez Is Only Beginning
summary: Mr. Chavez began organizing in San Jose, Calif., in the 1950s and once lived there. After revelations of sexual abuse by the labor leader, the city and his old neighborhood confront his legacy.

title: Inside Trump’s Secret Deal to Deport Migrants to Cameroon
summary: In Cameroon, the Trump administration found a partner it could pressure into accepting covertly deported migrants.

title: Somali Immigrants Have Effectively Been Denied a Fair Hearing, Lawsuit Says
summary: Two Minnesota-based legal groups accused the Justice Department of unconstitutionally expediting Somalis’ immigration cases.

title: The Brigade System Helps Restaurants Succeed. Does It Also Lead to Abuse?
summary: Allegations against Noma’s chef have spurred debate over whether a 19th-century model for organizing kitchen staffs breeds physical and psychic violence.

title: It Begins as a Tick Bite and Can Be Devastating. And It’s Spreading.
summary: The incidence of alpha-gal syndrome appears to be growing significantly. Patients who are bitten can develop a severe allergy to red meat, and a few have died.

title: What to Know About Alpha-Gal Syndrome, a Meat Allergy Linked to Ticks
summary: If you’re having an unexplained, intense reaction after eating beef or pork, it’s a good idea to seek a blood test.

title: Youth Program Hit by $17 Million Scam Had Prior Misconduct, Records Show
summary: New York City officials still have not fully explained how payroll cards from a summer jobs program were used to withdraw large amounts of cash from A.T.M.s last year.

title: Should These Abandoned Tracks Become a Park or a Train Line? Or Both?
summary: A disused rail line in Queens could help reconnect the borough and add much-needed park space. A new report suggests that a plan to do both is possible, but it faces long odds.

title: This Muscle Is the Unsung Hero of Longevity
summary: Building your butt muscles will help you stay injury free and independent in midlife and beyond.

title: There’s a Reason Air Travel Is Such a Mess
summary: There's a deeper story behind the turbulence in the airline industry.

title: For Putin, the War in Iran Changed Everything
summary: In one swoop, it overturned the conditions for conciliation.

title: Airline Anxiety
summary: We look at why flying right now is awful.

title: Late Night Questions the ‘Very Big Present’ Trump Got From Iran
summary: Josh Johnson was puzzled by what kind of gift the president could have received “from the people you are currently at war with.”

title: Two Men Arrested in Attack on Jewish Charity Ambulances in London
summary: The police said the men, aged 45 and 47, are accused of arson with intent to endanger life stemming from the arson attack on Monday in Golders Green.

title: Sarah Mullally, the First Female Archbishop of Canterbury, is About to Be Enthroned
summary: Sarah Mullally will be installed on Wednesday at Canterbury Cathedral. Her appointment to the role has been both celebrated, and denounced by some factions within the global Anglican Church.

title: Massachusetts Teacher Charged With Raping Two Students
summary: The students say the former teacher at Miss Hall’s, a boarding school, groomed them and began having sex with them at age 16, which state law considers old enough for consent.

title: Someone Left Their Heart at Grand Central, Captivating Commuters
summary: A heart-shaped balloon appeared on the ceiling of the main concourse around Valentine’s Day, giving rise to delight and theories about its origins.
